expectations.text: Runs pnpm sync or git diff to see what changed in upstream
expectations.evidence: Checks `git -C upstream/everything-claude-code rev-parse HEAD` vs pinned commit (both 841beea), then runs `diff claudekit/skills/coding-standards/SKILL.md upstream/everything-claude-code/skills/coding-standards/SKILL.md` confirming files are identical.

expectations.text: Explicitly applies cross-reference scan to the diff output (not just generic upgrade)
expectations.evidence: Step 2.5 section explicitly runs: `grep -rni "skill\|agent\|use_skill\|invoke\|require\|depends" "$COMP/"` and finds the real cross-reference on Line 15: `Use rules/common/coding-style.md when you need the shortest reusable rule layer instead of a full skill walkthrough.`

expectations.text: Describes what action to take if new cross-references ARE found in the diff
expectations.evidence: Finds the coding-style.md optional reference and provides two concrete options: Option A (vendor it with full sidecar template and cp command) and Option B (add a note to the sidecar). Correctly classifies as optional dependency via decision matrix.

expectations.text: Describes what action to take if NO new cross-references are found
expectations.evidence: Summary table row 'Other inter-skill dependencies: None → —' and closing statement: 'No mandatory dependencies were missed. The coding-standards skill is safe as-is.' Implicitly confirms sidecar.dependencies is accurate when no new refs found.
